Table 11: Violation rates without and with Sentinel enforcement, per model (all 8 models). Sentinel reduces violations by 59–95% across all models.

Table 18: Agent Deployment: 17 Agents across 7 Domains

Figure 2: Multi-agent violation rates across eight frontier LLMs with per-agent domain world models. Colors indicate vendor: Anthropic, Google, OpenAI. Even Claude Opus 4.6 violates 13.8%; GPT-5.4-mini reaches 97.5%. Dashed line shows 20% threshold.
Table 8: Latency Breakdown of Distributed Sentinel Verification Pipeline (Stage 2)
Table 16: Per-attack-category violation rate (%) for all 8 models

Figure 1: Distributed Sentinel architecture. Each domain contains an agent, sidecar, and local knowledge graph—forming isolated contextual islands. Sidecars perform local entity mapping and policy checking; cross-domain verification queries the STT source directly, returning only boolean results (True/False) to preserve data sovereignty. This boolean transparency ensures no raw data crosses domain boundaries.

Figure 3: Heatmap of violation rates (%) by attack category and model. Blue indicates low violation rates (safe), red indicates high rates (vulnerable), and white represents 50%. Key finding: side-channel inference shows uniformly high violation rates (60–85%) across all models except Opus, indicating this attack category is structurally invisible to per-agent world models. Gray cells (–) indicate unavailable evaluations.
Table 6: Recall by Attack Category (160 attack scenarios across 9 types)
